name: jainism

name: parsasm

name: confucianism

name: bible

name: qur'an

name: torah

name: hammer throw

name: javelin throw

name: decathlon

A player nominates the ball he is attempting to pot and into which pocket. He collects points for each successful hit. He continues until he makes a mistake. Before hitting the 15th ball, the 14 reds are set up again in the triangle.

definition: Races between two cyclists over two or three laps, with a timed sprint finish over the last 200m.

name: Keirin
definition: Sprint between six cyclists where a pacemaker on a moped gradually increases the pace over the first 1400m. When the pacemaker leaves the track the race develops into a sprint finish over the final 6-700m.

name: points race
definition: Cyclists aim to complete the highest number of laps. Bonus points are picked up in intermediate sprints held every 2500m.

name: Madison race
definition: A points race by teams comprising two cyclists in relay-format.

name: 500 m time trial
definition: Individual race against the clock over 500m for women.

name: 1 km time trial
definition: Individual race over 1000m against the clock for men.

name: one hour
definition: Competitors aim to complete as many kilometres as possible in one hour.

name: road race
definition: Road race with all the competitor starting en masse.

name: road time trial
definition: A race or stage against the clock with each cyclist setting off individually.

name: staging race
definition: A race disputed by teams with the classifications determined by the combined times over a day.

name: cyclo-cross
definition: Staged on various surfaces - earth, grass, sand or tarmac.

name: Vtt
definition: Mountain bikes

name: Vtt-cross
definition: An endurance race on mountain bikes around a 10km circuit over various terrain and with different degrees of slopes. Competitors are not allowed any exterior help.

name: Vtt-downhill
definition: Individual mountain bike race against the clock staged on a downhill circuit featuring obstacles.

name: bi-crossing
definition: A race where competitors have to cover a circuit with obstacles as quickly as possible.

name: trial
definition: A timed race in which competitors on mountain bikes must negotiate tricky terrain and obstacles without putting their feet down.

name: artistic cycling
definition: Individual or teams on one or two bicycles in an arena. Points are awarded by a jury.

name: cycle ball
definition: Competitive team sport using bicycles. One team against another with two persons per team. The aim is for competitors to move the ball using the front and rear wheels without letting their feet touch the ground.

name: dancing
definition: Dancing competition where couples are judged on technique and interpretation.

name: diving
definition: Competitors dive off a fixed or spring board and are assessed by seven judges giving marks up to ten for their acrobatic moves.

name: 10 m platform
definition: Divers take off from a ten metre high platform.

name: 10 m platform synchronised
definition: Two competitors from the same team dive off at the same time from separate platforms. As well as being judged on the standard criteria they are also awarded marks for synchronisation.

name: 3 m springboard
definition: Divers dive from a three-metre high springboard.

name: 3 m springboard synchronised
definition: Two competitors from the same team dive off at the same time from separate three-metre high springboards. As well as being judged on the standard criteria they are also awarded marks for synchronisation.

name: subaquatics
definition: Sports like hockey held under water

name: scuba diving
definition: Diving into different depths, with time measurement

name: equestrian
definition: Sport involving a horse and rider.

name: three-day event
definition: Equestrian event involving three disciplines - Dressage, cross-country and show jumping.

name: dressage
definition: A rider is judged on a series of moves at three different paces - walk, trot and gallop.

name: jumping
definition: A rider and horse attempt to jump a series of obstacles in an enclosed arena against the clock.

name: cross country
definition: Equestrian event held on a designated circuit in the countryside featuring artificial and natural obstacles.

name: fencing
definition: Combat sport using a sword or foil.

name: epee
definition: category of fencing using a weapon with a triangular cross-section blade and a large bell guard.

name: foil
definition: category of fencing using a weapon with a rectangular cross-section blade and a small bell guard.

name: sabre
definition: a fencing weapon with a flat blade and knuckle guard used with cutting or thrusting moves.

name: field Hockey
definition: A ball sport involving two teams of 11 players using curved sticks. The aim is to score as many goals as possible.

name: roll hockey
definition: Team sport similar to ice hockey, executed on 4 wheel roller skates with sticks and a ball

name: figure Skating
definition: To obtain the best marks possible from nine judges who award scores after two prepared sections - both skated to music - during which competitors must attempt to achieve the greatest possible harmony between artistic flair and technical precision.

name: singles
definition: The individual events comprise a short program which counts for 33.3% of the score, and the free skate which is worth 66.7%.

name: pairs
definition: The pairs event comprise a short program which counts for 33.3% of the score, and the free skate which is worth 66.7%.

name: ice dance
definition: Takes place over three days with, in order, two compulsory dances, the original dance (2min 30sec) and the free dance (4min).

name: freestyle Skiing
definition: Competitors perform a series of breathtaking acrobatic leaps from a prepared jump. Combined with moguls, jumps and ski ballet.

name: moguls
definition: Contestants attack a heavily moguled slope, performing technically challenging turns and jumps, while maintaining the most direct line from the top to the bottom of the hill.

name: aerials
definition: Competitors perform a series of aerial twists and turns from a prepared jump.

name: artistic skiing
definition: Competitors perform a series of artistic moves (jumps and twists) all set to music. The use of ski poles is permitted.

name: golf
definition: Competitors attempt to hit a small white ball with different clubs around a course of 18 holes varying in distance. The object is to make the fewest strokes possible.

name: gymnastics
definition: A sport consisting of a variety of disciplines in which gymnasts perform artistic and acrobatic moves on different apparatus. There are 6 disciplines for men and 4 for women.

name: floor exercise
definition: A gymnastics floor exercise on a 12m/sq matt.

name: vault
definition: gymnasts propel themselves by jumping on a trampoline after running quickly down a 25m lane

name: pommel horse
definition: Gymnasts perform an uninterrupted swing motion over a handled horse using balance and strength.

name: uneven bars
definition: Gymnasts run through an airborne routine swinging around and between the uneven bars.

name: parallel bars
definition: Gymnasts swing and turn with guile and grace around the parallel bars.

name: horizontal bar
definition: Gymnasts turn, spin and perform pirouettes around the horizontal bar.

name: rings
definition: Gymnasts perform feats of balance and strength from two suspended rings.

name: beam
definition: Gymnasts perform leaps and flips from the much feared balance beam just 10 cm wide.

name: rhythmic
definition: A mixture of dance and gymnastic moves.

name: clubs
definition: Dance and gymnastic moves whilst juggling clubs.

name: hoop
definition: A hoop is rolled and thrown whilst performing a dance routine.

name: ribbon
definition: Gymnasts work through a pre-arranged routine whilst manipulating a spectacular length of ribbon.

name: rope
definition: Jump and figure movements are performed while a rope is manipulated.

name: ball
definition: An exercise based on suppleness of the body using a ball.

name: trampoline
definition: Competitors perform complicated twists and turns whilst bouncing on the trampoline.

name: handball (team)
definition: A ball game using the hands contested by two teams of seven trying to throw the ball into the opponents goal.

name: horse racing, harness racing
definition: Mounted horse races.

name: flat racing
definition: A horse race over a flat course.

name: steeple chase
definition: A mounted horse race over at least eight classic obstacles, four of which must be different, such as the water jump, spruce fences, mud walls etc.

name: trotting
definition: Horses pitched to a jockeyed buggy race in a trot round a flat course.

name: cross country
definition: A long distance race including rustic obstacles such as real fences, streams and fallen trees.

name: ice hockey
definition: Two teams of six heavily padded skaters try and outscore each other by hitting a puck into the opponents goal.

name: National Hockey League (North American)
definition: Teams from the US and Canada make up the North American continents top league.

name: sledge hockey
definition: Like ice hockey but instead of skates small sledges are used. Competitors move by pushing with the arms

name: Jai Alai (Pelota)
definition: Because of the game organization, the proper identification of a specific game may require the combination of 2 subject details

name: fronton
definition: The *fronton* known as *Fronton Place Libre* consists of a rectangular outdoor court plus a large pink shaped wall at one end. The two sides and the other end are left opened.

name: jai-alai
definition: A *Jaï-Alai* is an indoor fronton with a 56 meters long court which is used exclusively to play *chistera-ancha*. Three walls are used in the game: the front, the back and the left hand wall.

name: left wall
definition: A *fronton with left hand wall* is an indoor fronton with a 36 meters long court where Pala or Main Nue are played. Three walls are used in the game: the front, the back and the left hand wall.

name: trinquet
definition: A *Trinquet* is an indoor fronton with a 28.5 meters long court surrounded by four walls. The four walls are used in the game.

name: rebot
definition: The *rebot* is the game played in a fronton *place libre* with 5 players in each team. They use a *chistera* (56 cm long and 12 cm large) or bare hands.

name: chistera ancha
definition: The *chistera-ancha* also called the *cesta punta* is the game played in a *jaï alai* with 1 or 2 players in each team. The players use a long curved basket called the *chistera* (68 cm long and 13 cm wide).

name: chistera corta
definition: The *chistera-corta* is the game played in a fronton *place libre* with left hand wall and 3 players in each team. The players use a long curved basket called the *chistera* (56 cm long and 12 cm wide).

name: bare hand
definition: The *Main Nue* (bare hand) is the ancestor of all Basque sports.

name: pala-ancha
definition: The *pala-ancha* is the game played in a fronton *place libre* with 2 players in each team. The players use a heavy wooden bat *the pala* (50 cm long and 20 cm wide).

name: pala-corta
definition: The *pala-corta* is the game played in a fronton with left hand wall and 2 players in each team. The players use a heavy wooden bat the *pala-corta* (50 cm long and 10 cm wide). The *pala* is the name for a heavy wooden bat.

name: pasaka
definition: The *pasaka* is the name of the game played in a trinquet by teams of 2 players facing each other on each side of a net where the pelota is hit between opponents.

name: xare
definition: The *xare*, is the name of the game played in a trinquet by teams of 2 players. The players use a *xare* (or the Argentina bat) 55 cm long and 16 cm wide.

name: judo
definition: A defensive martial art.

name: heavyweight

name: lightweight

definition: Two teams of six record points by hitting a ball over a net into the opponent's half of the court, keeping it in the air at all times. Points are won when opponents fail to return the ball.

name: beach volleyball
definition: Two teams of two players compete on a sand court. Unlike indoor volleyball, points are scored only when the serving team wins a rally or forces an error. The sand surface makes it more physically demanding than volleyball.

name: water polo
definition: Played in a pool between two teams of 7, who must stay afloat and can only use one hand to pass the ball or swim with it before trying to throw it into the opponents net to score goals.

name: water skiing
definition: On one or two skis contestants are pulled by a power boat along the water's surface

name: slalom
definition: Speed event where contestants round a course marked out by buoys

name: trick
definition: Skiers perform a maximum of figures in a limited time period

name: jump
definition: Skiers perform as long a jump as possible from a ramp in the water

name: combined
definition: A combined event made up of the slalom, jump and trick

name: weightlifting
definition: A strength test where competitors lift as heavy a weight as possible

name: snatch
definition: The bar must be lifted from floor to above the head in one movement

name: clean and jerk
definition: The bar is first lifted to the shoulders and then jerk it above the head and keep it there while maintaining straight legs

name: lake

name: river

name: land

name: wrestling
definition: Combat sport where each wrestler attempts to win over his adversary, by holding both shoulders on the ground (fall) long enough to be in control. Points can also be decisive.

name: freestyle
definition: A style where leg holds are allowed

name: greco-roman
definition: A style where the only holds allowed are those between the head and the belt

name: World games
definition: A competition featuring world class athletes

name: World Cup
definition: Sports meeting with competitors from the five continents

name: intercontinental cup
definition: Sports meeting bringing together competitors from different continents

name: continental cup
definition: Sports meeting where competitors come from a single continent

name: international cup
definition: Sports meeting where competitors come from several nations

name: National Cup
definition: Sports meeting where competitors come from a single nation

name: interregional cup
definition: Sports meeting where competitors come from several regions

name: regional cup
definition: Sports meeting where competitors come from a single region

name: league cup
definition: A competition within a sports league

name: world championship
definition: A championship where competitors from the five continents take part

name: intercontinental championship
definition: A championship where competitors from the different continents take part

name: continental championship 1st level
definition: A championship where top level competitors from a single continent take part

name: continental championship 2nd level
definition: A championship where second string competitors from a single continent take part

name: continental championship 3rd level
definition: A championship where third string competitors from a single continent take part

name: national championship 1st level
definition: A championship where top level competitors from a single nation take part

name: national championship 2nd level
definition: A championship where second string competitors from a single continent take part

name: national championship3rdlevel
definition: A championship where third string competitors from a single continent take part

name: national championship 4th level
definition: A championship where fourth string competitors from a single continent take part

name: regional championship
definition: A championship where competitors from a single region take part

name: Grand Prix
definition: High level sports competition, most often motor racing

name: intercontinental tournament
definition: Tournament where competitors from several continents take part

name: continental tournament
definition: Tournament where competitors from a single continent take part

name: international tournament
definition: Tournament where competitors from several nations take part

name: national tournament
definition: Tournament where competitors from a single nation take part

name: inter-nations competition
definition: Tournament where athletes from several nations take part

name: inter-clubs competition
definition: Tournament where competitors from several clubs take part

name: friendly competition
definition: Sports played but not in a tournament

name: all-stars competition
definition: Competition between teams made up of invited stars

name: exhibition
definition: demonstration

name: Super Bowl
definition: The American football final that determines the champion

name: paralympic games
definition: Olympic style Games for athletes with a disability

name: rodeo
definition: A discipline where wild horses or bulls must be mounted and mastered

name: barrel racing
definition: Rodeo discipline

name: calf roping
definition: Rodeo discipline

name: bull riding
definition: Rodeo discipline

name: bulldogging
definition: Rodeo discipline

name: saddle bronc
definition: Rodeo discipline

name: bareback
definition: Rodeo discipline

name: goat roping
definition: Rodeo discipline

name: mini golf sport
definition: A game where balls must be holed on an artificial course in as few strokes from the club as possible

name: bandy
definition: Played outdoors on ice. The size of the ice rink is about the size of a soccer field. Skates like ice hockey. A small hard ball is used for playing. Players play with sticks, much like in ice hockey, but the sticks are shorter and more rounded.

name: flying disc
definition: A group of events all played with a flying plastic disc. The exact regulation for the disc is different in different events but it has to be unbroken of solid plastic and a production model with no modification. Flying disc is sometimes called Frisbee.

name: ultimate
definition: Team game. Played outdoors on soccer-size field with 7 players per team and indoors with 5 players per team. Games are played to a preset number of goals. Goals are scored by passing the disc to a team-member catching it inside the goal area.

name: guts
definition: Team game where two teams of 5 players each face each other. Teams take turns to throw the disc towards the other team. Points are scored if the opposing team does not catch the disc.

name: overall
definition: Not an event in itself but in most competitions with individual events the winner of the competition will be awarded to the player with the highest combined score in the events.

name: distance
definition: The object is to throw the disc as far as possible. Each player get five throws per turn. Throws are made downwind. World record is over 200 meters.

name: discathon
definition: The object is to throw and run a park course of about 1 kilometre. Four players compete in each heat. They have two discs each. They may also carry a third disc in reserve. Where one thrown disc stops the second disc must be thrown.

name: DDC
definition: Abbreviation for Double Disc Court. Two players per team in a square 13x13 metre. The teams start with one disc per team and throw at each other. Discs are thrown back and forth until one or both hit the ground.

name: SCF
definition: Abbreviation for Self Caught Flights. Divided into two sub events called MTA (Maximum Time Aloft) and TRC (Throw Run Catch). In both sub events the player throws a disc and then runs to catch it with one hand before it hits the ground.

name: freestyle
definition: A judged event where groups of two or three players perform as many tricks as possible. In competitions the judges can use either a ranking system or set scores on presentation, execution and difficulty.

name: accuracy
definition: Each player has 28 throws from various distances and angles to throw the disc through a goal 1.5 x 1.5 meters. World record is 25 of 28 possible.

name: disc golf
definition: Much like golf but played with discs instead of balls and clubs. Instead of a hole in the ground there is a metal basket with light metal chains to throw the disc at. Players often carry discs specialized for long throws, right or left bends.

name: floorball
definition: Played indoors in a court the size of a basketball court. 6 players per team, of which one is a who plays in a kneeling position without a stick like in ice hockey. The other players use plastic clubs and a light plastic ball to pass and shoot goals.

name: casting
definition: Using fishing equipment to hit a target and score points

name: tug-of-war
definition: Two teams pulling against each other on a rope

name: croquette
definition: Using mallets to hit a ball through hoops

name: dog racing
definition: Dogs racing around a track

name: sled
definition: Harnessed to a sled

name: oval track
definition: Pursuing an artificial rabbit around an oval track

name: skeleton
definition: In skeleton the competitor lays on his/hers stomach when racing down the course. The competitor must be on the sled when crossing the finishing line. The total time of all races in a competition is added together.

name: Australian rules football
definition: Football played under Australian rules

name: Canadian football
definition: Football played under Canadian rules

name: duathlon
definition: Duathlon

name: hornuss
definition: Swiss team sport with 16 or 18 men per team. A rubber puck is hit towards the field of the adversary team which tries to hit the puck in the air with a wooden board thrown in the air

name: fist ball
definition: Men's team sport similar to volleyball (with a much lower net) executed mainly in gymnastics clubs

name: inline skating
definition: Competitive sports using inline skates

name: grass ski
definition: Alpine skiing on roller skis and grass

name: snowbiking
definition: Individual sport on snow on a snow bike, the athlete has two mini skis, previous name: ski bob

name: twirling
definition: Team competition with majorettes and a jury

name: kendo
definition: Japanese traditional martial art using a bamboo sword, sometimes called Japanese fencing. (Programme of National Sports Festival in Japan)

name: jukendo
definition: Japanese traditional martial art using a model rifle made of wood. *Juken* means a rifle or gun with blade (bayonet). (Programme of National Sports Festival in Japan)

name: naginata
definition: Japanese traditional martial art using a pole sword made of wood. *Naginata* means a spear with a curved blade. (Programme of National Sports Festival in Japan)

name: kyudo
definition: Japanese traditional martial art using a bamboo bow and arrows, sometimes called Japanese archery. (Programme of National Sports Festival in Japan)

name: kabaddi
definition: The attacking side scores by touching, and the side to guard scores by capturing. The attacker continues calling it *kabaddi*.

name: sepak takraw
definition: The game like volleyball which must not use a hand. The ball made of rattan is used. (Programme of National Sports Festival in Japan)

name: wushu
definition: Generally, Chinese traditional martial art. (Programme of National Sports Festival in Japan)

name: darts
definition: Two players compete by throwing metal darts at a wall-mounted circular board divided radially into segments of different scores

name: bodybuilding
definition: A person displays pronounced muscle tone and exaggerated muscle mass and definition for overall aesthetic effect

name: sports disciplinary action
definition: Actions, including fines and suspensions levied by sports organisations and teams

name: sports awards
definition: Awards and honors given by sports organisations or won by sports figures

name: unrest, conflicts and war
definition: Acts of socially or politically motivated protest and/or violence.

name: act of terror
definition: Act of violence, often deadly, designed to raise fear and anxiety in a population

name: armed conflict
definition: Disputes between opposing groups involving the use of weaponry, but not formally declared a war

name: civil unrest
definition: Dissatisfaction among the general population as evidenced by rallies, strikes, demonstrations or sabotage

name: revolutions
definition: An often violent change in a nation's system of government by internal forces that results in systematic social change.

name: rebellions
definition: Armed uprising by citizens of a nation with the intent to overthrow the government, without necessarily achieving social change

name: political dissent
definition: Disagreement between political groups, usually organized and sometimes resulting in imprisonment of the dissenters

name: religious conflict
definition: Conflicts involving religious differences

name: social conflict
definition: Conflicts involving social differences

name: coup d'etat
definition: The overthrow of an established government by an organized group, often the military or a political party

name: guerrilla activity
definition: Anti-government actions by clandestine groups using hit-and-run techniques or sabotage, kidnapping and the like

name: bioterrorism
definition: Use of biological agent to raise the level of fear within a population, whether deaths occur or not.

name: bombings
definition: Use of explosive devices against people, buildings or structures

name: massacre
definition: The death of a large group of people over a brief period of time

name: genocide
definition: Systematic killing of one clan, tribe or ethnic type by another

name: riots
definition: Violent, destructive demonstrations often involving injury to individuals and destruction of property

name: demonstration
definition: A public show of feeling or opinion, as by a mass meeting or parade

name: war
definition: A formal declaration of hostilities by one country against another

name: civil war
definition: Armed conflict between members of the same nation or geographical region, in some cases with a desire to divide the nation or region.

name: international military intervention
definition: Temporary use of international forces by invitation or by a decision of an outside body in another country or region for the purpose of resolving a crisis.

name: prisoners and detainees
definition: People captured and imprisoned by their enemy during war or armed conflict

name: conflict (general)
definition: A dispute which can be at several levels,

name: peacekeeping force
definition: Varied national military forces under unified international command to maintain order in disputed areas.

name: crisis
definition: Conflict of all kinds that rises to a level where, depending on events, governments can fall, war be declared or stock markets crash, or not

name: weaponry
definition: Armaments of all types used to conduct war

name: weather
definition: The study, reporting and prediction of meteorological phenomena.

name: forecast
definition: Prediction of the course of the weather in the future either near term or long term

name: global change
definition: The determination whether the Earth is getting warm, or colder, whether seas are rising or falling and the like

name: report
definition: Any distributed announcement about the weather

name: weather news
definition: General information about weather events

name: statistic
definition: Numerical facts about the weather such as temperature, barometric pressure, river levels, humidity, high and low tides and the like

name: warning
definition: Alerts to the general populace about severe weather coming their way
